---
title: "age_youth_Meta analysis: mean level change and rank order Stability for sequential"
author: "Prerana Chandratre"
date: "13/07/2025"
output: html_document
---

```{r Load packages}
library(readxl)             # Loads package for reading Excel files
library(metafor)            # Loads metafor package for conducting meta-analyses
library(psych)              # Loads psych package for descriptive statistics and psychometrics
library(ggplot2)            # Loads ggplot2 for creating visualizations
library(patchwork)          # Loads patchwork to combine multiple ggplots into one
library(dplyr)

theme_update(              # Updates global ggplot2 theme settings
  plot.title = element_text(hjust = 0.5),            # Center-aligns plot titles
  axis.text.x = element_text(color="black"),        # Sets x-axis text color to black
  axis.text.y = element_text(color="black")         # Sets y-axis text color to black
)

```


```{r Data transformation}
data_aw <- read_xlsx("output/long_format_with_newcol.xlsx")  # Loads the dataset from Excel

head(data_aw)
```

```{r Validation for accurate Output}
# Sanity Check on Key Columns
cols_to_check <- c("1_ID", "1.1_CaseID", "2_Author", "3_Year", "10_Size", "11_Mage", "16_CNT", "17_Eth", "18_Female","Negative/Positive+LS", "interval", "timepoints", "WBmi", "WBsdi", "WBmj", "WBsdj", "WBcorrij", "WBtitj",  "33_Relt1", "34_Relt")

# Check if these columns exist in the data and print their types
for (col in cols_to_check) {
  if (col %in% names(data_aw)) {
    cat(col, ":", class(data_aw[[col]]), "\n")
  } else {
    cat(col, ": NOT FOUND in data\n")
  }
}

```

```{r convert data type into numeric format}
data_aw$`1.1_CaseID` <- as.numeric(data_aw$`1.1_CaseID`)            # Converts article ID to factor (categorical)
data_aw$`2_Author` <- as.factor(data_aw$`2_Author`)          # Converts author name to factor
data_aw$`11_Mage` <- as.numeric(data_aw$`11_Mage`)
data_aw$`16_CNT` <- as.factor(data_aw$`16_CNT`)
data_aw$`17_Eth` <- as.factor(data_aw$`17_Eth`)
data_aw$`18_Female` <- as.numeric(data_aw$`18_Female`)
data_aw$`Negative/Positive+LS` <- as.numeric(data_aw$`Negative/Positive+LS`)
data_aw$`33_Relt1` <- as.numeric(data_aw$`33_Relt1`)
data_aw$`34_Relt` <- as.numeric(data_aw$`34_Relt`)

```

```{r Adding unique ID for each row and count of CaseID}

# Step 1: Add RowID and CaseID_Count
data_aw <- data_aw %>%
  mutate(RowID = row_number()) %>%
  group_by(`1.1_CaseID`) %>%
  mutate(CaseID_Count = n()) %>%
  ungroup()

# Step 2: Reorder columns to place RowID and CaseID_Count after X1.1_CaseID
data_aw <- data_aw %>%
  select(
    '1_ID', '1.1_CaseID', RowID, CaseID_Count, everything()
  )


head(data_aw)

```



```{r Data cleaning and summary for mean age}
# Step 1: Filter out NAs and 9999 to calculate mean
clean_mage <- data_aw %>%
  filter(!is.na(`11_Mage`) & `11_Mage` != 9999)

# Step 2: Calculate rounded mean (2 digits)
mean_mage <- round(mean(clean_mage$`11_Mage`, na.rm = TRUE), 2)
cat("Mean of Mage (rounded to 2 decimals):", mean_mage, "\n")

# Step 3: Replace 9999 with rounded mean
data_aw <- data_aw %>%
  mutate(`11_Mage` = ifelse(`11_Mage` == 9999, mean_mage, `11_Mage`))

#Check summary
summary(data_aw$`11_Mage`)

```

```{r considering only positive affect data }
# Filter rows where Negative/Positive+LS == 0 and extract 1_IDs
valid_ids <- data_aw %>%
  filter(`Negative/Positive+LS` == 0) %>%
  pull(`1_ID`)

#cat("Filtered IDs where LS == 0:\n")
#print(valid_ids)
```

```{r Seperating Country Column into World Region based on Country}
# Add World Region based on country (16_CNT)
data_aw <- data_aw %>%
  mutate(region = case_when(
    `16_CNT` %in% c("U.S.", "Canada") ~ "North America",
    `16_CNT` %in% c("UK", "France", "Germany", "Italy", "Spain", "Netherlands", "Sweden", "Austria", "Belgium", "Croatia", "England", "Finland",  "Finnland", "Greek", "Norway", "Portugal", "Romania", "Russia", "Scotland", "Slovakia", "Spain", "Sweden", "Swiss", "Switzerland" , "Switzerland & Norway", "UK") ~ "Europe",
    `16_CNT` %in% c("India", "China","Hong Kong", "Hong Kong, China", "Japan", "South Korea","Korea", "Taiwan", "Indonesia", "Vietnam", "Israel") ~ "Asia",
    `16_CNT` %in% c("Brazil", "Argentina", "Mexico", "Chile", "Colombia", "Maxico") ~ "Latin America",
    `16_CNT` %in% c("South Africa", "Nigeria", "Kenya", "Egypt") ~ "Africa",
    `16_CNT` %in% c("Australia", "New Zealand") ~ "Oceania",
    `16_CNT` %in% c("Canada", "U.S.") ~ "North America",
    TRUE ~ "Other"
  ))

table(data_aw$'16_CNT')

table(data_aw$region)
```

[World_Bank](https://datahelpdesk.worldbank.org/knowledgebase/articles/906519-world-bank-country-and-lending-groups)

```{r Seperating Country Column intoEconomic Status based on Country }
# Add World Bank Income Level to data_aw
data_aw <- data_aw %>%
  mutate(income_level = case_when(

    # High-income countries
`16_CNT` %in% c("United States", "U.S.", "Germany", "France", "Canada", "Australia", 
                "UK", "United Kingdom", "Japan", "South Korea", "Netherlands", 
                "Sweden", "Italy", "Spain", "Switzerland", "Austria", "Denmark", 
                "Finland","Finnland", "Ireland", "Norway", "New Zealand", "Singapore",
                "Belgium", "Croatia", "England", "Greece", "Portugal", "Scotland", 
                "Slovakia", "Swiss", "Switzerland & Norway", "Hong Kong", 
                "Hong Kong, China","Greek" ,"Taiwan", "Israel", "Chile", "Korea") ~ "High income",

# Upper-middle income countries
`16_CNT` %in% c("Brazil", "China", "Mexico", "South Africa", "Colombia", "Thailand",
                "Argentina", "Indonesia", "Turkey", "Malaysia", "Peru", "Iran", 
                "Russia", "Romania", "Costa Rica", "Panama", "Maxico") ~ "Upper middle income",

# Lower-middle income countries
`16_CNT` %in% c("India", "Kenya", "Vietnam", "Nigeria", "Philippines", "Ghana", 
                "Bangladesh", "Pakistan", "Zambia", "Egypt", "Sri Lanka", "Cameroon") ~ "Lower middle income",

# Low-income countries
`16_CNT` %in% c("Uganda", "Mozambique", "Ethiopia", "Burundi", "Mali", "Chad", 
                "South Sudan", "Malawi", "Togo", "Rwanda", "Yemen, Rep.") ~ "Low income",

    TRUE ~ "Unknown"
  ))


table(data_aw$`region`)
table(data_aw$`income_level`)
```



```{r Data Cleaning and summary for Timelag}
# Step 1: Filter out NAs and 9999 to calculate mean
clean_timelag <- data_aw %>%
  filter(!is.na(`WBtitj`) & `WBtitj` != 9999)

# Step 2: Calculate rounded mean (2 digits)
mean_timelag <- round(mean(clean_timelag$`WBtitj`, na.rm = TRUE), 2)
cat("Mean of timelag (rounded to 2 decimals):", mean_timelag, "\n")

# Step 3: Replace 9999 with rounded mean
data_aw <- data_aw %>%
  mutate(`WBtitj` = ifelse(`WBtitj` == 9999, mean_timelag, `WBtitj`))

# Optional: Check summary
summary(data_aw$`WBtitj`)

```


```{r Relaability Data cleaning and adding avg column}

# Clean column: 33_Relt1
clean_relt1 <- data_aw %>%
  filter(!is.na(`33_Relt1`) & `33_Relt1` != 9999)

mean_relt1 <- round(mean(clean_relt1$`33_Relt1`, na.rm = TRUE), 2)
cat("Mean of 33_Relt1 (rounded to 2 decimals):", mean_relt1, "\n")

data_aw <- data_aw %>%
  mutate(`33_Relt1` = ifelse(is.na(`33_Relt1`) | `33_Relt1` == 9999, mean_relt1, `33_Relt1`))

# Clean column: 34_Relt
clean_relt <- data_aw %>%
  filter(!is.na(`34_Relt`) & `34_Relt` != 9999)

mean_relt <- round(mean(clean_relt$`34_Relt`, na.rm = TRUE), 2)
cat("Mean of 34_Relt (rounded to 2 decimals):", mean_relt, "\n")

data_aw <- data_aw %>%
  mutate(`34_Relt` = ifelse(is.na(`34_Relt`) | `34_Relt` == 9999, mean_relt, `34_Relt`))

# Optional: Check summaries
summary(data_aw$`33_Relt1`)
summary(data_aw$`34_Relt`)


# Calculating Avg for each ID and adding in new column
# Step 1: Calculate row-wise average of 33_Relt1 and 34_Relt
data_aw <- data_aw %>%
  mutate(avg_rel = rowMeans(select(., `33_Relt1`, `34_Relt`), na.rm = TRUE))

# Step 2: Replace NA or NaN in avg_rel with overall mean (rounded to 2 digits)
mean_avg_rel <- round(mean(data_aw$avg_rel, na.rm = TRUE), 2)

data_aw <- data_aw %>%
  mutate(avg_rel = ifelse(is.na(avg_rel) | is.nan(avg_rel), mean_avg_rel, avg_rel))

# Step 3: Round final values and relocate
data_aw <- data_aw %>%
  mutate(avg_rel = round(avg_rel, 2)) %>%
  relocate(avg_rel, .after = `34_Relt`)

```


```{r regression and quadratic effects for mean}
# Year of birth (yob) = Year at T1 minus mean age
# dat <- transform(dat, yob = `3_Year` - `WBmt1`) Birth year?

# Center and scale age at T1 (mean age)

data_aw <- transform(data_aw, agem1ic1 = (data_aw$`11_Mage` - 13.72) / 1)

# Square of centered-scaled age
#data_aw <- transform(data_aw, agem1ic2 = WBmi^2)
data_aw <- transform(data_aw, agem1ic2 = agem1ic1^2)

# Cube of centered-scaled age
#data_aw <- transform(data_aw, agem1ic3 = WBmi^3)
data_aw <- transform(data_aw, agem1ic3 = agem1ic1^3)

# Center time lag
data_aw <- transform(data_aw, timelagc = WBtitj -  0.99) # WBtitj - mean of timelag # in NArcissism they also have -ve timelags in some cases

# Estimate age at T2 as age at T1 + time lag
data_aw <- transform(data_aw, agem2i = (`11_Mage`) + WBtitj)

# Convert percent female to proportion
#dat <- transform(dat, female = dat$`X18_Female` / 100) # as we already have in % in original data

```


```{r Computation of Effect Sizes of Mean-Level Change}
# Step 1: Calculate mean correlation (excluding NA and 9999)
data_aw$WBcorrij[data_aw$WBcorrij %in% c(9999, "9999", "", NA)] <- NA
data_aw$WBcorrij <- as.numeric(data_aw$WBcorrij)
data_aw$corrmean <- mean(data_aw$WBcorrij, na.rm = TRUE)

# Step 2: Replace missing correlations with the mean
data_aw$corrx <- ifelse(is.na(data_aw$WBcorrij), data_aw$corrmean, data_aw$WBcorrij)

# Step 3: Compute effect size (Cohen’s d) as difference between mean2 and mean1 divided by SD1
# (mean2 = WBmj, mean1 = WBmi, SD1 = WBsdi)
data_aw <- transform(data_aw, es_d = (WBmj - WBmi) / WBsdi)

# Step 4: Annualized effect size: divide by time lag
data_aw <- transform(data_aw, es_dyear = es_d / WBtitj)

# Step 5: Compute variance of d
data_aw <- transform(data_aw, v_d = ((2 * (1 - corrx) / `10_Size`) + (es_d^2 / (2 * `10_Size`))))

# Step 6: Annualized variance
data_aw <- transform(data_aw, v_dyear = v_d / (WBtitj^2))

data_aw
```

```{r Checking Descriptive anaylsis for Estimated effect size}
summary(data_aw$es_d)
```

```{r Data cleaning and replacing none values for Female column}
# Replacing 9999 values from Female % column
# Step 1: Filter out NAs and 9999 to calculate mean
clean_female <- data_aw %>%
  filter(!is.na(`18_Female`) & `18_Female` != 9999)

# Step 2: Calculate rounded mean (2 digits)
mean_female <- round(mean(clean_female$`18_Female`, na.rm = TRUE), 2)
cat("Mean of X18_Female (rounded to 2 decimals):", mean_female, "\n")

# Step 3: Replace 9999 with rounded mean
data_aw <- data_aw %>%
  mutate(`18_Female` = ifelse(`18_Female` == 9999, mean_female, `18_Female`))

# Optional: Check summary
summary(data_aw$`18_Female`)

```

```{r Summary Statistics for timepoints and Gender}
summary(data_aw$timepoints)
summary(data_aw$`18_Female`)

```

```{r  Calculate standardized mean change}
library(metafor)

# Make sure your data is in the proper format
dat_meta <- data_aw %>%
  rename(
    mean1 = WBmi,         # baseline mean
    mean2 = WBmj,         # follow-up mean
    sd1   = WBsdi,        # baseline SD
    n     =`10_Size`,     # sample size
    r     = WBcorrij,     # correlation  # r = WBcorrij
    study = `1_ID`        # unique ID for study/case
  ) %>%
  mutate(
    n = suppressWarnings(as.numeric(n))  # convert n into numeric for calculations
  )


# Calculate standardized mean change with raw score standardization
dat_meta_escalc <- escalc(
  measure = "SMCR",
  m1i = mean2,      # follow-up mean
  m2i = mean1,      # baseline mean
  sd1i = sd1,       # baseline SD
  ni = n,           # sample size
  ri = r,           # correlation
  data = dat_meta,
  #slab = study      # study label
)

head(dat_meta_escalc)
```


```{r fit random-effects model and examine results}
# Filter out rows with missing yi or vi
dat_meta_complete <- dat_meta_escalc %>%
  filter(!is.na(yi) & !is.na(vi))

res <- rma(yi, vi, data=dat_meta_complete)
res
```


```{r Subset by happiness factors}
# Subset data for Positive/ Life Satisfaction (0)
dat.positive <- subset(data_aw, subset = data_aw$`Negative/Positive+LS` == 0)

# Subset data for Negative Life Satisfaction (1)
dat.negative <- subset(data_aw, subset = data_aw$`Negative/Positive+LS` == 1)

```


```{r Calcuting number of positive and Negative values}
sum(data_aw$`Negative/Positive+LS` == 0)
sum(data_aw$`Negative/Positive+LS`== 1)
```


```{r Subsets and Filtering Based on Life Satisfaction}

# Subset data for Positive/ Life Satisfaction (0)
dat.positive2 <- subset(data_aw, subset = data_aw$`Negative/Positive+LS` == 0)

# Subset data for Negative Life Satisfaction (1)
dat.negative2 <- subset(data_aw, subset = data_aw$`Negative/Positive+LS` == 1)

# Keep only non-missing yearly effect sizes (es_dyear)
dat.positive2.es_dyear <- subset(dat.positive2, !is.na(dat.positive2$es_dyear))
dat.negative2.es_dyear <- subset(dat.negative2, !is.na(dat.negative2$es_dyear))

```


```{r check overall data summary}
summary(dat.positive2)
```
```{r checking number of values in each timelag}
table(data_aw$WBtitj)
```

```{r splitting timelag into small and big}
# ============================================================
# Create subsets for different timelag categories (AgeWell data)
# ============================================================

# Convert timelag to numeric if needed
data_aw$WBtitj <- as.numeric(data_aw$WBtitj)

# 3 months or less (≈ 0.25 years)
data_aw_timelag_3m <- subset(data_aw, WBtitj <= 0.25)

# 6 months or less (≈ 0.5 years)
data_aw_timelag_short <- subset(data_aw, WBtitj <= 0.5)

# More than 6 months (long-term)
data_aw_timelag_long <- subset(data_aw, WBtitj > 0.5)

# Optional: Check counts
cat("Count of cases with timelag ≤ 3 months:", nrow(data_aw_timelag_3m), "\n")
cat("Count of cases with timelag ≤ 6 months:", nrow(data_aw_timelag_short), "\n")
cat("Count of cases with timelag > 6 months:", nrow(data_aw_timelag_long), "\n")

```


```{r Checking Ethnecity in the data}
table(data_aw$`17_Eth`)
```

```{r Splitting Ethnecity into White and Other}
# Subset: White only
data_aw_white <- subset(data_aw, `17_Eth` == "White/European")

# Subset: All others
data_aw_other <- subset(data_aw, `17_Eth` != "White/European")

# Count White
white_count <- count(data_aw_white)
cat("Count of participants identified as White/European:", white_count$n, "\n")

# Count Other
other_count <- count(data_aw_other)
cat("Count of participants identified as Other ethnicity:", other_count$n, "\n")

```

```{r multilevel random effect code: for positive effect}


res <- rma.mv(yi=es_dyear, V=v_dyear, random = ~ 1 | `1.1_CaseID`/RowID, data=dat.positive2) 
#changed here with unique ID


res
res$b
```


```{r multilevel random effect code for negative effect}

res <- rma.mv(yi=es_dyear, V=v_dyear, random = ~ 1 | `1.1_CaseID`/RowID, data=dat.negative2) #changed here with unique ID

res
res$b
```


```{r three-level random effect model with between study variances }
#res_model2<- rma.mv(yi=es_dyear, V=v_dyear, 
                 #  random = ~ 1 | X1.1_CaseID, data=dat.positive)

#res
                  
```


```{r Visualization: Hypothetical Linear Slopes for Each Trait}
library(ggplot2)

dat.mlachange <- data.frame(x = c(9.50, 19))
# Creates a minimal data frame with age range from 9.25 to 20 to use as x-axis base

ggplot(dat.mlachange, aes(x, y)) +
  
  labs(x = "Age", y = "Cumulative d value") +
  scale_x_continuous(breaks = seq(2,6,1), lim = c(9.50, 19), expand = c(0, 0)) + # should we take 2,3,6? because timelag min max values
  # Sets x-axis breaks every 10 years, limits from 9 to 20 with no margin padding
  
  scale_y_continuous(breaks = seq(-0.8, 0.2, 0.2), lim = c(-0.8, 0.8), expand = c(0, 0)) + # Which one should we consider?
  # Sets y-axis breaks and limits for cumulative d-values from -0.8 to 0.2
  
  geom_hline(yintercept = 0, linetype = "dashed", color = "black", linewidth = 1) +
  geom_abline(intercept = -0.03947127 * -9.50, slope = -0.0395, linetype = "solid", color = "black", linewidth = 1) + # for positive # -9.50 is min of age
  
  geom_abline(intercept = 0.02703036 * -9.50, slope = 0.0270, linetype = "solid", color = "black", linewidth = 1) + # for negative
  
  annotate("text", x = 70, y = -0.2, label = "Positive/LS") +
  annotate("text", x = 69, y = -0.3, label = "Negative") +
  theme(panel.grid.major = element_blank(),
        panel.grid.minor = element_blank(),
        panel.background = element_blank(),
        axis.line = element_line(colour = "black"))

# Adds text annotations labeling each trajectory at different y-values

#ggsave(file="fig1.eps", width=5, height=3.75)
# Saves the plot as an EPS file with specified dimensions
```

```{r Creating Subsamples based on Correlations}

# Correlation-based subsamples
dat.positive.corr <- subset(dat.positive, subset = corrx != "NA")
dat.negative.corr <- subset(dat.negative, subset = corrx != "NA")

```


```{r Data Cleaning For Size Column}
# Step 1: Filter out NAs and 9999 to calculate mean
clean_size <- data_aw %>%
  filter(!is.na(`10_Size`) & `10_Size` != 9999)

# Step 2: Calculate rounded mean (2 digits)
mean_size <- round(mean(clean_size$`10_Size`, na.rm = TRUE), 2)
cat("Mean of X10_Size (rounded to 2 decimals):", mean_size, "\n")

# Step 3: Replace 9999 with rounded mean
data_aw <- data_aw %>%
  mutate(`10_Size` = ifelse(`10_Size` == 9999, mean_size, `10_Size`))

# Optional: Check summary
summary(data_aw$`10_Size`)

```
```{r Saving final processed data for further model use}
# Load library
library(writexl)

# Save the processed data_aw to Excel
write_xlsx(data_aw, "output/processed_data_aw.xlsx")

```


```{r Female Proportion Descriptive Stats}
mean(data_aw$`18_Female`, na.rm = TRUE)  
# Mean proportion of females across samples (NA values ignored)

min(data_aw$`18_Female`, na.rm = TRUE)  
# Minimum proportion of females

max(data_aw$`18_Female`, na.rm = TRUE)  
# Maximum proportion of females

sd(data_aw$`18_Female`, na.rm = TRUE)  
# Standard deviation of female proportion

median(data_aw$`18_Female`, na.rm = TRUE)  
# Median proportion of females

```


```{r Descriptive Statistics for Time Lag}
describe(data_aw$timepoints, skew=TRUE)  # timepoints
# Produces detailed descriptive stats for time lag, including skewness (via `psych::describe`)

describe(data_aw$WBtitj, skew=TRUE)  # timelag by codebook 
```

```{r Multilevel Analysis: mean level change for positive and negative affect}
# Effect Size Analyses
res <- rma.mv(yi=es_dyear, V=v_dyear, random = ~ 1 | `1.1_CaseID`/RowID, data=dat.positive) #updated unique RowID
# Fits a multilevel random-effects model for positive factor
#   - yi: annualized effect size (d per year)
#   - V: corresponding variance
#   - random effects: nested structure (caseid within sampid)


print(res, digits=3)
# Prints model results with 3 decimal places

cat("Total number of people based on sample size for positive affect:", 
    sum(dat.positive$`10_Size`, na.rm = TRUE), "\n")
# Total number of participants used in positive effect size analysis (sample-level)

res <- rma.mv(yi=es_dyear, V=v_dyear, random = ~ 1 | `1.1_CaseID`/RowID, data=dat.negative) #updated unique RowID
# Same as above, but for negative factor

print(res, digits=3)
# Outputs model results

cat("Total number of people based on sample size for negative affect:", 
    sum(dat.negative$`10_Size`, na.rm = TRUE), "\n")
# Total participants in negative group

```
```{r Multilevel Analysis: mean level change for small and large time lag}
# Effect Size Analyses
# Also do for +ve and -ve also PAY ATTENTION
res <- rma.mv(yi=es_dyear, V=v_dyear, random = ~ 1 | `1.1_CaseID`/RowID, data=data_aw_timelag_short)
# Fits a multilevel random-effects model for positive factor
#   - yi: annualized effect size (d per year)
#   - V: corresponding variance
#   - random effects: nested structure (caseid within sampid)


print(res, digits=3)
# Prints model results with 3 decimal places

res <- rma.mv(yi=es_dyear, V=v_dyear, random = ~ 1 | `1.1_CaseID`/RowID, data=data_aw_timelag_long)
# Same as above, but for negative factor

print(res, digits=3)
# Outputs model results


```


```{r Age Effects: Modeling the effect of age on yearly change for positive factor}

res <- rma.mv(yi=es_dyear, V=v_dyear, random = ~ 1 | `1.1_CaseID`/RowID,
               data=dat.positive, mods = cbind(agem1ic1))
# Positive: meta-regression with linear effect of age (centered and scaled)

print(res, digits=4)
# Print results with 4 decimal places

res <- rma.mv(yi=es_dyear, V=v_dyear, random = ~ 1 | `1.1_CaseID`/RowID,
              data=dat.positive, mods = cbind(agem1ic1, agem1ic2))
# Positive: includes both linear and quadratic age terms

print(res, digits=4)
# Display results

res <- rma.mv(yi=es_dyear, V=v_dyear, random = ~ 1 | `1.1_CaseID`/RowID,
               data=dat.positive, mods = cbind(agem1ic1, agem1ic2, agem1ic3))
# Positive: adds cubic age term

print(res, digits=4)
# Output for 3rd-order polynomial model

```
```{r Age Effects: Modeling the effect of age on yearly change for Negative factors:}

# Model 1: Age effect with 1 linear moderator
res <- rma.mv(yi = es_dyear, V = v_dyear, 
              random = ~ 1 | `1.1_CaseID`/RowID,   # Random effects: nested structure (RowID within CaseID)
              data = dat.negative, 
              mods = cbind(agem1ic1))             # Include first age-related moderator (linear term)
print(res, digits = 4)   # Print results with 4 decimal places


# Model 2: Age effect with linear + quadratic moderator
res <- rma.mv(yi = es_dyear, V = v_dyear, 
              random = ~ 1 | `1.1_CaseID`/RowID,   # Same nested random-effects structure
              data = dat.negative, 
              mods = cbind(agem1ic1, agem1ic2))   # Add quadratic (squared) age term as moderator
print(res, digits = 4)


# Model 3: Age effect with linear + quadratic + cubic moderators
res <- rma.mv(yi = es_dyear, V = v_dyear, 
              random = ~ 1 | `1.1_CaseID`/RowID,   # Same nested random-effects structure
              data = dat.negative, 
              mods = cbind(agem1ic1, agem1ic2, agem1ic3))  # Add cubic age term as moderator
print(res, digits = 4)

```


```{r Age Effects: Modeling the effect of age on yearly change for small time lag}

# Model 1: Linear age effect for short time lags
res <- rma.mv(yi = es_dyear, V = v_dyear, 
              random = ~ 1 | `1.1_CaseID`/RowID,   # Random effects: accounts for nested structure (rows within cases)
              data = data_aw_timelag_short,       # Subset of data with small/short time lags
              mods = cbind(agem1ic1))             # Linear age moderator
print(res, digits = 4)   # Print results with 4 decimal places


# Model 2: Linear + quadratic age effects for short time lags
res <- rma.mv(yi = es_dyear, V = v_dyear, 
              random = ~ 1 | `1.1_CaseID`/RowID,   # Same random-effects structure
              data = data_aw_timelag_short, 
              mods = cbind(agem1ic1, agem1ic2))   # Include quadratic (squared) age moderator
print(res, digits = 4)


# Model 3: Linear + quadratic + cubic age effects for short time lags
res <- rma.mv(yi = es_dyear, V = v_dyear, 
              random = ~ 1 | `1.1_CaseID`/RowID,   # Same random-effects structure
              data = data_aw_timelag_short, 
              mods = cbind(agem1ic1, agem1ic2, agem1ic3))  # Add cubic age moderator
print(res, digits = 4)
```


```{r Age Effects: Modeling the effect of age on yearly change for long time lag}
# Model 1: Linear age effect for long time lags
res <- rma.mv(yi = es_dyear, V = v_dyear, 
              random = ~ 1 | `1.1_CaseID`/RowID,   # Random effects: nested structure (rows within cases)
              data = data_aw_timelag_long,        # Subset of data with long time lags
              mods = cbind(agem1ic1))             # Linear age moderator
print(res, digits = 4)   # Print results with 4 decimal places


# Model 2: Linear + quadratic age effects for long time lags
res <- rma.mv(yi = es_dyear, V = v_dyear, 
              random = ~ 1 | `1.1_CaseID`/RowID,   # Same random-effects structure
              data = data_aw_timelag_long, 
              mods = cbind(agem1ic1, agem1ic2))   # Add quadratic (squared) age moderator
print(res, digits = 4)


# Model 3: Linear + quadratic + cubic age effects for long time lags
res <- rma.mv(yi = es_dyear, V = v_dyear, 
              random = ~ 1 | `1.1_CaseID`/RowID,   # Same random-effects structure
              data = data_aw_timelag_long, 
              mods = cbind(agem1ic1, agem1ic2, agem1ic3))  # Add cubic age moderator
print(res, digits = 4)
```


```{r Correlation  Analysis: wellbeing + ethnicity} 

# Subset based on Positive Affect group (LS = 0)
#dat.positive <- subset(data_aw, Negative.Positive.LS == 0)   # Keep only rows classified as Positive Affect

# Then subset within Positive group by ethnicity
dat.positive_white <- subset(dat.positive, `17_Eth` == "White/European")   # Positive Affect + White/European subgroup
dat.positive_other <- subset(dat.positive, `17_Eth` != "White/European")   # Positive Affect + all other ethnicities

# Ensure WBmi and WBmj are numeric (convert if they are factors/characters)
dat.positive_white$WBmi <- as.numeric(as.character(dat.positive_white$WBmi))
dat.positive_white$WBmj <- as.numeric(as.character(dat.positive_white$WBmj))

dat.positive_other$WBmi <- as.numeric(as.character(dat.positive_other$WBmi))
dat.positive_other$WBmj <- as.numeric(as.character(dat.positive_other$WBmj))

# Run correlation tests  White/European group
cat("White group  Correlation: % Female vs WBmi\n")
print(cor.test(dat.positive_white$`18_Female`, dat.positive_white$WBmi))   # Correlation between %Female and WBmi

cat("\n White group Correlation: % Female vs WBmj\n")
print(cor.test(dat.positive_white$`18_Female`, dat.positive_white$WBmj))   # Correlation between %Female and WBmj

# Run correlation tests Other ethnicities group
cat("\n Other ethnicities Correlation: % Female vs WBmi\n")
print(cor.test(dat.positive_other$`18_Female`, dat.positive_other$WBmi))   # Correlation between %Female and WBmi

cat("\n Other ethnicities Correlation: % Female vs WBmj\n")
print(cor.test(dat.positive_other$`18_Female`, dat.positive_other$WBmj))   # Correlation between %Female and WBmj

```


```{r correlation analysis: effect size + ethnicity + wellbeing}

# Subset based on Positive Affect group (LS = 0)
#dat.positive <- subset(data_aw, Negative.Positive.LS == 0)

# Subset by ethnicity
dat.positive_white <- subset(dat.positive, `17_Eth` == "White/European")
dat.positive_other <- subset(dat.positive, `17_Eth` != "White/European")

# Ensure numeric conversion of variables (if necessary)
dat.positive_white$WBmi <- as.numeric(as.character(dat.positive_white$WBmi))
dat.positive_white$WBmj <- as.numeric(as.character(dat.positive_white$WBmj))
dat.positive_white$`10_Size` <- as.numeric(as.character(dat.positive_white$`10_Size` ))

dat.positive_other$WBmi <- as.numeric(as.character(dat.positive_other$WBmi))
dat.positive_other$WBmj <- as.numeric(as.character(dat.positive_other$WBmj))
dat.positive_other$`10_Size`  <- as.numeric(as.character(dat.positive_other$`10_Size` ))

# Run correlations White group
#cat(" White group Correlation: Sample Size vs WBmi\n")
#print(cor.test(dat.positive_white$X10_Size, dat.positive_white$WBmi))

#cat("\n White group Correlation: Sample Size vs WBmj\n")
print(cor.test(dat.positive_white$`10_Size` , dat.positive_white$WBmj))

# Run correlations Other group
#cat("\n Other ethnicities Correlation: Sample Size vs WBmi\n")
print(cor.test(dat.positive_other$`10_Size` , dat.positive_other$WBmi))

#cat("\n Other ethnicities Correlation: Sample Size vs WBmj\n")
print(cor.test(dat.positive_other$`10_Size` , dat.positive_other$WBmj))


```


```{r Displaying Boxplot}
# Increase bottom margin to fit rotated labels
par(mar = c(8, 4, 4, 2) + 0.1)

# Boxplot for WBmi
boxplot(WBmi ~ region, data = dat.positive, 
        main = "Mean i", 
        xlab = "", 
        las = 2,            # Rotate labels perpendicular to axis
        cex.axis = 0.8)     # Shrink label text size

# Boxplot for WBmj
boxplot(WBmj ~ region, data = dat.negative, 
        main = "Mean j", 
        xlab = "", 
        las = 2, 
        cex.axis = 0.8)

# Boxplot for effect size
boxplot(es_d ~ region, data = dat.positive, 
        main = "Effect Size", 
        xlab = "", 
        las = 2, 
        cex.axis = 0.8)

```


```{r Multivariate Moderator Models for Mean-Level Change}
# Positive effect size model
res_pos <- rma.mv(yi = es_dyear, V = v_dyear,
                  random = ~ 1 | `1.1_CaseID`/RowID,
                  data = dat.positive,
                  mods = ~ `18_Female` + factor(region))
cat("\n  Positive Moderator Model \n")
print(res_pos, digits = 4)

# Negative effect size model
res_neg <- rma.mv(yi = es_dyear, V = v_dyear,
                  random = ~ 1 | `1.1_CaseID`/RowID,
                  data = dat.negative,
                  mods = ~ `18_Female` + factor(region))
cat("\n Negative Moderator Model \n")
print(res_neg, digits = 4)


```


```{r Moderator Meta-Analyses for positive and Negative affects}
#  Moderator Meta-Analyses 
# Explore whether gender, region, economic status, and timelag explain variability in:
# - Mean-level change (es_dyear)
# - Well-being at time 1 (WBmi) and time 2 (WBmj)

# Step 1: Create subsets for positive and negative affect groups
dat.positive <- subset(data_aw, `Negative/Positive+LS` == 0 & 
                       !is.na(`18_Female`) & 
                       !is.na(region) & 
                       !is.na(income_level) & 
                       !is.na(WBtitj))

dat.negative <- subset(data_aw, `Negative/Positive+LS` == 1 & 
                       !is.na(`18_Female`) & 
                       !is.na(region) & 
                       !is.na(income_level) & 
                       !is.na(WBtitj))

# Step 2: Convert categorical moderators to numeric (for correlation tests)
dat.positive$region_num <- as.numeric(as.factor(dat.positive$region))
dat.positive$income_level_num <- as.numeric(as.factor(dat.positive$income_level))

dat.negative$region_num <- as.numeric(as.factor(dat.negative$region))
dat.negative$income_level_num <- as.numeric(as.factor(dat.negative$income_level))

# Step 3: Correlations for Positive Affect group
cat("Positive Affect Group:\n")
cat("Correlation: Gender (% Female) vs Income Level\n")
print(cor.test(dat.positive$`18_Female`, dat.positive$income_level_num))

cat("\nCorrelation: Gender (% Female) vs Region\n")
print(cor.test(dat.positive$`18_Female`, dat.positive$region_num))

cat("\nCorrelation: Gender vs Timelag\n")
print(cor.test(dat.positive$`18_Female`, dat.positive$WBtitj))

cat("\nCorrelation: Sample Size vs T1 Well-being (WBmi)\n")
print(cor.test(dat.positive$`18_Female`, dat.positive$WBmi))

cat("\nCorrelation: Sample Size vs T2 Well-being (WBmj)\n")
print(cor.test(dat.positive$`18_Female`, dat.positive$WBmj))

cat("\nCorrelation: Gender vs Mean-Level Change (es_dyear)\n")
print(cor.test(dat.positive$`18_Female`, dat.positive$es_dyear))

# Step 4: Correlations for Negative Affect group
cat("\n Negative Affect Group:\n")
cat("Correlation: Gender (% Female) vs Income Level\n")
print(cor.test(dat.negative$`18_Female`, dat.negative$income_level_num))

cat("\nCorrelation: Gender (% Female) vs Region\n")
print(cor.test(dat.negative$`18_Female`, dat.negative$region_num))

cat("\nCorrelation: Gender vs Timelag\n")
print(cor.test(dat.negative$`18_Female`, dat.negative$WBtitj))

cat("\nCorrelation: Sample Size vs T1 Well-being (WBmi)\n")
print(cor.test(dat.negative$`10_Size`, dat.negative$WBmi))

cat("\nCorrelation: Sample Size vs T2 Well-being (WBmj)\n")
print(cor.test(dat.negative$`10_Size`, dat.negative$WBmj))

cat("\nCorrelation: Gender vs Mean-Level Change (es_dyear)\n")
print(cor.test(dat.negative$`18_Female`, dat.negative$es_dyear))

```


```{r Multilevel meta-analysis for timelag only}
#  Multilevel meta-analysis for timelag positive

res_pos <- rma.mv(yi = es_dyear, V = v_dyear,
                  random = ~ 1 | `1.1_CaseID`/RowID,
                  data = dat.positive,
                  mods = ~  WBtitj)

cat("\n Multilevel model Positive Affect:\n")
print(res_pos, digits = 4)


#negative only timelag
res_pos <- rma.mv(yi = es_dyear, V = v_dyear,
                  random = ~ 1 | `1.1_CaseID`/RowID,
                  data = dat.negative,
                  mods = ~  WBtitj)

cat("\n Multilevel model Positive Affect:\n")
print(res_pos, digits = 4)
```



```{r Multilevel meta-analysis for Gender, Region, Income level and Timelag}
library(metafor)

#Multilevel meta-analysis including all moderators
# Positive group
res_pos <- rma.mv(yi = es_dyear, V = v_dyear,
                  random = ~ 1 | `1.1_CaseID`/RowID,
                  data = dat.positive,
                  mods = ~ `18_Female` + factor(region) + factor(income_level) + WBtitj)

cat("\n Multilevel model Positive Affect:\n")
print(res_pos, digits = 4)



# Negative group
res_neg <- rma.mv(yi = es_dyear, V = v_dyear,
                  random = ~ 1 | `1.1_CaseID`/RowID,
                  data = dat.negative,
                  mods = ~ `18_Female` + factor(region) + factor(income_level) + WBtitj)

cat("\n Multilevel model Negative Affect:\n")
print(res_neg, digits = 4)

```


```{r Linear regression for female and mean level change for positive affect}
library(ggplot2)

ggplot(dat.positive, aes(x = `18_Female`, y = es_dyear)) +
  geom_point(alpha = 0.6, color = "#2c3e50") +
  geom_smooth(method = "lm", se = TRUE, color = "#2980b9") +
  labs(
    title = "Effect Size vs. % Female (Positive Affect Group)",
    x = "% Female in Sample",
    y = "Mean-Level Change (es_dyear)"
  ) +
  theme_minimal()

```


```{r  Linear regression for female and effect size for positive affect}
ggplot(dat.positive, aes(x = `18_Female`, y = es_dyear, size = `10_Size`)) +
  geom_point(alpha = 0.6, color = "#2c3e50") +
  geom_smooth(method = "lm", se = TRUE, color = "#2980b9") +
  labs(
    title = "Effect Size vs. % Female",
    x = "% Female",
    y = "Effect Size",
    size = "Sample Size"
  ) +
  theme_minimal()

```

```{r random-effects meta-analysis on Positive Affect data}

# Fit a random-effects meta-analysis model on Positive Affect data
res <- rma(yi = es_dyear, vi = v_dyear, 
           random = ~ 1 | `1.1_CaseID`/RowID,   # Random effects: rows nested within cases
           data = dat.positive)

# Perform Egger's regression test for funnel plot asymmetry (publication bias check)
regtest(res)

```


```{r Positive Publication Bias}
# Positive Publication Bias

# Step 1: Standard random-effects meta-analysis
res <- rma(yi = es_dyear, vi = v_dyear, data = dat.positive)
# Random-effects model using the simpler `rma()` (not multilevel) on Positive data

print(res, digits = 3)

# Step 2: Egger's test for publication bias
regtest(res)
# Egger's regression test for funnel plot asymmetry (publication bias)

# Step 3: Multilevel meta-regression with publication status as moderator (Q: is year correct? to consider publication bias ? they use published/unpublished status
res_mv <- rma.mv(yi = es_dyear, V = v_dyear,
                 random = ~ 1 | `1.1_CaseID`/RowID,
                 data = dat.positive,
                 mods = ~ `3_Year` )
# Multilevel model with publication status (X3_year) as moderator

print(res_mv, digits = 3)

# Step 4: Frequency table of publication status
table(dat.positive$`3_Year`)

```



```{r Positive Affect: Funnel plot}

# Fit a simple random-effects meta-analysis model for Positive Affect group
res <- rma(yi = es_dyear, vi = v_dyear, 
           random = ~ 1 | `1.1_CaseID`/RowID,   # Random effects: rows nested within cases
           data = dat.positive)
# Simple model for Positive group

print(res, digits = 3)   # Print results rounded to 3 decimals


# Test for funnel plot asymmetry (small-study effects / publication bias)
regtest(res)   # Eggers regression test


# Plot funnel plot for Positive Affect group
funnel(res, 
       xlab = "Effect Size (d per year)",      # X-axis: effect size
       ylab = "Standard Error",                # Y-axis: study precision
       main = "Positive Affect")               # Title of plot

# Add custom text annotation on the plot (e.g., "P" for Positive)
mtext("P", side = 3, line = 2.4, adj = -0.16, cex = 1.0)  
# You can also add values like p-value or Eggers test result here, e.g.:
# mtext(paste0("Egger p = ", round(regtest(res)$pval, 3)), side = 3, line = 2.4, adj = 0, cex = 0.8)


# Fit a multilevel model with moderator (Year of study)
res <- rma.mv(yi = es_dyear, V = v_dyear, 
              random = ~ 1 | `1.1_CaseID`/RowID,   # Random nested effects
              data = dat.positive, 
              mods = cbind(`3_Year`))             # Include publication year as moderator

# Positive: multilevel model with publication year moderator
print(res, digits = 3)   # Print model with moderator effect


# Check distribution of Year variable in Positive group
table(dat.positive$`3_Year`)   # Frequency table of publication years

```


```{r Negative Affect : Funnel plot}
# Fit a simple random-effects meta-analysis model for Negative Affect group
res <- rma(yi = es_dyear, vi = v_dyear,  
           random = ~ 1 | `1.1_CaseID`/RowID,   # Random effects: rows nested within cases
           data = dat.negative)
# Simple model for Negative group

print(res, digits = 3)   # Print results rounded to 3 decimals


# Test for funnel plot asymmetry (publication bias / small-study effects)
regtest(res)   # Eggers regression test


# Plot funnel plot for Negative Affect group
funnel(res, 
       xlab = "Effect Size (d per year)",   # X-axis: effect sizes
       ylab = "Standard Error",             # Y-axis: measure of precision
       main = "Negative Affect")            # Title of the funnel plot

# Add custom text annotation "N" (for Negative) above the plot
mtext("N", side = 3, line = 2.4, adj = -0.16, cex = 1.0)  
# You can also add values like Eggers test p-value here, for example:
# mtext(paste0("Egger's p = ", round(regtest(res)$pval, 3)), side = 3, line = 1.5, adj = 0, cex = 0.8)


# Fit a multilevel model with moderator (publication year) for Negative group
res <- rma.mv(yi = es_dyear, V = v_dyear, 
              random = ~ 1 | `1.1_CaseID`/RowID,   # Random nested effects
              data = dat.negative, 
              mods = cbind(`3_Year`))              # Publication year as moderator

# Negative: multilevel model with publication year moderator
print(res, digits = 3)   # Print model results with moderator effect


# Show frequency table of publication years in the Negative group
table(dat.negative$`3_Year`)   # Distribution of study years
```


```{r Both Positive and Negative Funnel plots}
# Create multiple plots (e.g., in a 3-row layout)
par(mfrow = c(2, 1)) 

# Positive funnel
res1 <- rma(yi = es_dyear, vi = v_dyear, data = dat.positive)
funnel(res1, main = "Positive")

# Negative funnel
res2 <- rma(yi = es_dyear, vi = v_dyear, data = dat.negative)
funnel(res2, main = "Negative")


# Now record the full set of plots
fig2 <- recordPlot()

```


```{r Save Combined Funnel Plots}
# Create the plot (e.g., funnel plot)
res <- rma(yi = es_dyear, vi = v_dyear, data = dat.positive)
funnel(res, xlab = "Effect Size (d per year)", ylab = "SE")

fig2 <- recordPlot()
# Stores the current plotting device (all funnel plots) in memory

# Just display the stored plot in the running environment
fig2

```



```{r Aggregate values for Lollipop graph}
# --- Aggregate values for Lollipop graph (5-year bins) ---
agg_df <- data_aw %>%
  mutate(decade = floor(as.numeric(`3_Year`) / 5) * 5) %>%
  group_by(region, decade) %>%
  summarise(
    mean_es = mean(es_dyear, na.rm = TRUE),
    n       = dplyr::n(),
    .groups = "drop"  # avoids message and ungroups safely
  ) %>%
  mutate(
    decade = as.factor(decade),
    effect = ifelse(mean_es >= 0, "Positive", "Negative")
  )


```


```{r Lollipop graph: based on region}
# --- Lollipop graph: mean effect per year by region and 5-year bin ---
ggplot(agg_df, aes(x = decade, y = mean_es, label = round(mean_es, 2))) +
  geom_segment(aes(xend = decade, y = 0, yend = mean_es), color = "black") +
  geom_point(aes(color = effect), size = 3) +
  geom_text(
    color = "white", size = 2.5,
    vjust = ifelse(agg_df$mean_es > 0, 1.5, -0.5)
  ) +
  facet_grid(effect ~ region, scales = "free_y", space = "free_y") +
  labs(
    title = "Mean Effect per Year by Region and 5-Year Bin",
    subtitle = "Effect direction shown by color and facets",
    x = "5-Year Bin",
    y = "Mean Effect per Year"
  ) +
  theme_bw() +
  theme(
    strip.text = element_text(size = 9, face = "bold"),
    axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 45, hjust = 1),
    legend.position = "none"
  )


```




```{r Lollipop graph: based on income level}
# --- Prepare data with effect per year and decade binning ---
res_df <- dat_meta_complete %>%
  mutate(
    effect = ifelse(es_dyear >= 0, "Positive", "Negative"),
    decade = floor(as.numeric(`X3_Year`) / 10) * 10
  ) %>%
  filter(!is.na(decade), !is.na(income_level), !is.na(es_dyear))

# --- Aggregate mean effect per year by income level and decade ---
agg_df <- res_df %>%
  group_by(income_level, effect, decade) %>%
  summarise(
    mean_es = mean(es_dyear, na.rm = TRUE),
    n       = dplyr::n(),
    .groups = "drop"
  ) %>%
  mutate(decade = as.factor(decade))

# --- Lollipop plot: mean effect per year by income level and decade ---
ggplot(agg_df, aes(x = decade, y = mean_es, label = round(mean_es, 2), color = effect)) +
  geom_segment(aes(xend = decade, y = 0, yend = mean_es), color = "black") +
  geom_point(size = 3) +
  geom_text(
    color = "white", size = 2.5,
    vjust = ifelse(agg_df$mean_es > 0, 1.5, -0.5)
  ) +
  facet_grid(effect ~ income_level, scales = "free_y", space = "free_y") +
  labs(
    title = "Mean Effect Sizes by Income Level and Decade",
    subtitle = "Effect direction based on es_dyear (no z-score)",
    x = "Decade",
    y = "Mean Effect per Year"
  ) +
  theme_bw() +
  theme(
    strip.text = element_text(size = 9, face = "bold"),
    axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 45, hjust = 1),
    legend.position = "none"
  )

```


```{r Computation of Effect Sizes of Rank-Order Stability}

# Adjust correlations for reliability (disattenuated correlation) # we decided to skip rank order stability for now
data_aw$corrdis <- with(data_aw, data_aw$WBcorrij / data_aw$avg_rel) #rel?
data_aw$corrdis <- ifelse(data_aw$corrdis >= 0.99, 0.99, data_aw$corrdis)  # Cap extreme values

# Fisher z transformation
data_aw$corrdis_z <- fisherz(data_aw$corrdis)  # or use metafor::fisherz()

# Variance of z (The -3 comes from the mathematical derivation of the sampling distribution of the Fisher z-transformed correlation. When you compute the standard error of the transformed z-score, it accounts for:

#The fact that correlations have a non-normal distribution (especially when 
#r is large or small)
#The Fisher z-transformation stabilizes the variance
#The degrees of freedom adjustment happens as a result of how the variance of z is estimated from the correlation sampling distribution
#It corrects for the bias in small samples.
#It ensures the variance estimate is valid under the Fisher transformation.)

data_aw$v_corr <- 1 / (data_aw$`10_Size` - 3)
data_aw$v_corrdis <- data_aw$v_corr / (data_aw$avg_rel*data_aw$avg_rel)

# Compute 95% Confidence Intervals in z-space
data_aw$ci_lower_z <- data_aw$corrdis - 1.96 * data_aw$v_corrdis
data_aw$ci_upper_z <- data_aw$corrdis + 1.96 * data_aw$v_corrdis

# Back-transform to r (correlation space)
data_aw$ci_lower_r <- fisherz2r(data_aw$ci_lower_z)
data_aw$ci_upper_r <- fisherz2r(data_aw$ci_upper_z)

```



```{r Rank Order Stability: model for Positive affect}
#For Positive affects
dat.positive <- subset(data_aw, subset = data_aw$`Negative/Positive+LS` == 0)
res <- rma.mv(yi=corrdis, V=v_corrdis, random = ~ 1 | `1.1_CaseID`/RowID, data=dat.positive)
print(res, digits=3)

fisherz2r(0.619  )
lower_bound <- (0.619  -1.96 *0.011)
upper_bound <- (0.619  +1.96 *0.011)

cat("Confidence interval: lower =", lower_bound, "\n")
cat("Confidence interval: upper =", upper_bound, "\n")

```

```{r for fisherz2r}
library(psych)  # for fisherz2r

# Values
val1   <- fisherz2r(0.5504314)
val_lo <- fisherz2r(0.59744   )  # lower
val_hi <- fisherz2r(0.64056   )  # upper

# Print them nicely
cat("Main value :", val1, "\n")
cat("Lower bound:", val_lo, "\n")
cat("Upper bound:", val_hi, "\n")


```

```{r Rank Order Stability: model for Negative affect}
#For negative affects
dat.negative <- subset(data_aw, subset = data_aw$`Negative/Positive+LS` == 1)
res <- rma.mv(yi=corrdis, V=v_corrdis, random = ~ 1 | `1.1_CaseID`/RowID, data=dat.negative)
print(res, digits=3)

fisherz2r(0.558)
lower_bound <- (0.558- 1.96 *0.035) #lower bound
upper_bound <- (0.558+ 1.96 *0.035) # upper_bound

cat("Confidence interval: lower =", lower_bound, "\n")
cat("Confidence interval: upper =", upper_bound, "\n")
```

```{r for fisherz2r}
library(psych)  # for fisherz2r

# Values
val1   <- fisherz2r(0.506492)
val_lo <- fisherz2r(0.4894   )  # lower
val_hi <- fisherz2r(0.6266   )  # upper

# Print them nicely
cat("Main value :", val1, "\n")
cat("Lower bound:", val_lo, "\n")
cat("Upper bound:", val_hi, "\n")


```

```{r Effect Size Analyses}

#For Positive affects
res <- rma.mv(yi=corrdis, V=v_corrdis, random = ~ 1 | `1.1_CaseID`/RowID, data=dat.positive)
print(res, digits=3)

sum(dat.positive.corr$X10_Size)

```
`

```{r}

# For Negative affects
res <- rma.mv(yi=corrdis, V=v_corrdis, random = ~ 1 | `1.1_CaseID`/RowID, data=dat.negative)
print(res, digits=3)

sum(dat.negative.corr$`10_Size`)
```



```{r Age Effects}
# for Positive

res <- rma.mv(yi=corrdis, V=v_corrdis, random = ~ 1 | `1.1_CaseID`/RowID, data=dat.positive, mods = cbind(agem1ic1))
print(res, digits=4)

res <- rma.mv(yi=corrdis, V=v_corrdis, random = ~ 1 | `1.1_CaseID`/RowID, data=dat.positive, mods = cbind(agem1ic1, agem1ic2))
print(res, digits=4)

res <- rma.mv(yi=corrdis, V=v_corrdis, random = ~ 1 | `1.1_CaseID`/RowID, data=dat.positive, mods = cbind(agem1ic1, agem1ic2, agem1ic3))
print(res, digits=4)
```

```{r Age Effects}
# For Negative
res <- rma.mv(yi=corrdis, V=v_corrdis, random = ~ 1 | `1.1_CaseID`/RowID, data=dat.negative, mods = cbind(agem1ic1))
print(res, digits=4)

res <- rma.mv(yi=corrdis, V=v_corrdis, random = ~ 1 | `1.1_CaseID`/RowID, data=dat.negative, mods = cbind(agem1ic1, agem1ic2))
print(res, digits=4)

res <- rma.mv(yi=corrdis, V=v_corrdis, random = ~ 1 | `1.1_CaseID`/RowID, data=dat.negative, mods = cbind(agem1ic1, agem1ic2, agem1ic3))
print(res, digits=4)

```

```{r Moderator Analyses: 1}
# For positive and Negative Female + Effect size
res <- rma.mv(yi=corrdis, V=v_corrdis, random = ~ 1 | `1.1_CaseID`/RowID, data=dat.positive, mods = cbind(`18_Female`, timelagc))
print(res, digits=4)

res <- rma.mv(yi=corrdis, V=v_corrdis, random = ~ 1 | `1.1_CaseID`/RowID, data=dat.negative, mods = cbind(`18_Female`, timelagc))
print(res, digits=4)

```


```{r Moderator Analyses: 2}
res <- rma.mv(yi=corrdis, V=v_corrdis, random = ~ 1 | `1.1_CaseID`/RowID, data=dat.positive, mods = `18_Female`)
print(res, digits=4)

# For P and N, Female + mean age
res <- rma.mv(yi=corrdis, V=v_corrdis, random = ~ 1 | `1.1_CaseID`/RowID, data=dat.positive, mods = cbind(`18_Female`, agem1ic1, timelagc))
print(res, digits=4)

res <- rma.mv(yi=corrdis, V=v_corrdis, random = ~ 1 | `1.1_CaseID`/RowID, data=dat.negative, mods = cbind(`18_Female`, agem1ic1, timelagc))
print(res, digits=4)
```


```{r Moderator Analyses: 3}
# For P and N, Female + ethnicity
res <- rma.mv(yi=corrdis, V=v_corrdis, random = ~ 1 | `1.1_CaseID`/RowID, data=dat.positive, mods = cbind(`18_Female`, `17_Eth`, timelagc))
print(res, digits=4)

res <- rma.mv(yi=corrdis, V=v_corrdis, random = ~ 1 | `1.1_CaseID`/RowID, data=dat.negative, mods = cbind(`18_Female`, `17_Eth`, timelagc))
print(res, digits=4)
```


```{r Moderator Analyses: 4}
# For P and N, Female + income (Q: How to do for char variable?)
res <- rma.mv(yi=corrdis, V=v_corrdis, random = ~ 1 | `1.1_CaseID`/RowID, data=dat.positive, mods = cbind(`18_Female`, factor(income_level), timelagc))
print(res, digits=4)

res <- rma.mv(yi=corrdis, V=v_corrdis, random = ~ 1 | `1.1_CaseID`/RowID, data=dat.negative, mods = cbind(`18_Female`, factor(income_level), timelagc))
print(res, digits=4)
```


```{r Effect Size Analyses Controlled for Time Lag}
#For Positive
res <- rma.mv(yi=corrdis, V=v_corrdis, random = ~ 1 | `1.1_CaseID`/RowID, data=dat.positive, mods = cbind(timelagc))
print(res, digits=3)


#For Negative
res <- rma.mv(yi=corrdis, V=v_corrdis, random = ~ 1 | `1.1_CaseID`/RowID, data=dat.negative, mods = cbind(timelagc))
print(res, digits=3)

sum(dat.negative.corr$`10_Size`)


```


```{r Age Effects Controlled for Time Lag}

#cor.test(data_aw$WBmi, data_aw$WBtitj)

# For Positive
res <- rma.mv(yi=corrdis, V=v_corrdis, random = ~ 1 | `1.1_CaseID`/RowID, data=dat.positive, mods = cbind(agem1ic1, timelagc))
print(res, digits=4)

res <- rma.mv(yi=corrdis, V=v_corrdis, random = ~ 1 | `1.1_CaseID`/RowID, data=dat.positive, mods = cbind(agem1ic1, agem1ic2, timelagc))
print(res, digits=4)

res <- rma.mv(yi=corrdis, V=v_corrdis, random = ~ 1 | `1.1_CaseID`/RowID, data=dat.positive, mods = cbind(agem1ic1, agem1ic2, agem1ic3, timelagc))
print(res, digits=4)

# For Negative
res <- rma.mv(yi=corrdis, V=v_corrdis, random = ~ 1 | `1.1_CaseID`/RowID, data=dat.negative, mods = cbind(agem1ic1, timelagc))
print(res, digits=4)

res <- rma.mv(yi=corrdis, V=v_corrdis, random = ~ 1 | `1.1_CaseID`/RowID, data=dat.negative, mods = cbind(agem1ic1, agem1ic2, timelagc))
print(res, digits=4)

res <- rma.mv(yi=corrdis, V=v_corrdis, random = ~ 1 | `1.1_CaseID`/RowID, data=dat.negative, mods = cbind(agem1ic1, agem1ic2, agem1ic3, timelagc))
print(res, digits=4)
```
